main_thesis: The Huanan Seafood Market cluster provides negligible evidence for zoonotic origin because seafood markets repeatedly formed early clusters during COVID-zero periods, making HSM not a remarkable coincidence under lab leak.
sub_theses: The conditional probability p(HSM|Lab Leak,Wuhan) is much higher than commonly estimated (>1% rather than 0.01-0.1%) because seafood markets repeatedly became outbreak epicenters during COVID-zero periods
The conditional probability p(HSM|Zoonosis,Wuhan) is lower than assumed (3-5%) because markets were just one of many possible spillover locations and HSM was not unique in Wuhan
When HSM evidence is correctly evaluated as <5x likelihood ratio rather than 10,000x, lab leak becomes the leading hypothesis since all other evidence favors lab leak

The Thailand December 2020 seafood market outbreak with a shrimp vendor index case demonstrates that something special about market environments produces early clusters independent of zoonotic spillover
methodological_stance: Strict Bayesian likelihood ratio approach requiring careful estimation of conditional probabilities p(Evidence|Hypothesis,Background) rather than naive population-based calculations; uses empirical patterns from COVID-zero market clusters as calibration data for conditional probabilities.
key_questions_addressed: Does the early cluster of COVID-19 cases at Huanan Seafood Market constitute strong evidence for zoonotic origin versus being explicable under lab leak?
What is the conditional probability of observing the HSM cluster given a pandemic started in Wuhan under each hypothesis?
How should seafood market clusters observed during later COVID-zero periods update beliefs about the HSM cluster?
textual_anchors: seafood markets and facilities repeatedly form early clusters
index case there was also a shrimp vendor
less than 5x – Because, as explained in 1, we need to divide the two
